ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Continued To Jun-23-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for the Co-Trustees.

Ms. Callejo reported that the transferring of the final asset of the trust has been completed in February, that it has been received by the trust and deposited, that all records have been forwarded to Mr. Harris to complete the accounting, it should be complete in 1 month, that the co-trustees live in England and it is difficult to obtain signatures and that the petition should be on file by the end of June. The status on final distribution was continued to June 23, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that it will be on file prior to this date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lopes. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-10-2015 Continued To May-12-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for Co-Trustees.

Ms. Callejo stated that receipts for partial distribution were filed, that the last account from Fidelity was collected last week, that they just deposited the funds (a little over $100,000.00) and requested a three month continuance to do the taxes and a final accounting. Status on final distribution was continued to May 12, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Saephan. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-18-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for Petitioners/Co-Trustees.

Ms. Callejo stated that she would like to have partial distribution, costs and fees addressed today and that they have been trying to obtain information from England regarding Patrick's Estate. Ms. Callejo also stated that she can get the Daughters to sign 13100 declarations, that the Fidelity account is very time consuming but has been appreciating in value, spoke of issues regarding Megan Carpenter's distribution and suggested a six month status. The Court suggested opening a separate IOLTA (Interest on Lawyer Trust Account) account for Megan's distribution. The accounting petition was taken under submission. Status on final distribution was set for February 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Callejo will prepare a revised order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (153 Gladstone Drive, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Oct-22-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Called with case number 287152. Franchesca Callejo appearing for petitioners. Sale is confirmed at $495,000.00. Additional bond in the amount of $470,000.00 is required. Counsel will have to wait until her client signs the bond before filing it. She will submit an order.
In the conservatorship matter, the assessment fee is paid. Ms. Callejo has been working with the Public Guaradian regarding the Social Security payments. Mr. Blackman is in court and he stated he has also tried it is not resolved. Ms. Callejo will prepare a declaration regarding that issue. Hearing on a written status report is set on 12/17. Accounting is approved. Ms. Callejo will prepare an order.
In the trust matter, Ms. Callejo has obtained couple of original statements for the Public Guardian. The Drifuss statement is still missing. The IRA account with New York Life was not inventory. She will need a supplemental inventory. Trust accounting is continued to 10/22.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto CSR# 5229. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.
